name: Rice, Apple and Raisin Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, heat the oil over high heat until very hot, about 2 minutes. Add the onions and bell peppers; saute about 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Add the pecans (substitute hickory nuts if needed) and continue cooking for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add the raisins and butter together so the raisins absorb as much butter as possible. Stir until butter is melted, then cook until raisins are plump, about 4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Add the rice and seasoning mix and cook until rice starts looking frizzly (a bit like Krispy Treats). Chef Prudhomme recommended using converted rice. Lucy used brown, long grain rice - super!. This will require about 2 to 3 minutes, stirring almost constantly before the rice looks "frizzly".
6. Stir in the stock, scraping pan bottom well, then stir in the apples.
7. Cover pan and bring to boil; lower heat and simmer covered for 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let sit, COVERED, until rice is tender and stock is absorbed, about 30 minutes.
8. Serve immediately, allowing about 3/4 cup per person.
